'''Frithia''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="GCG" /> located in the [[Frithia system/Legends|Frithia system]], a part of the [[Glythe sector/Legends|Glythe sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Diet/Legends|predator]] known as [[poth]] as well as the gigantic herd creature [[Krag (creature)/Legends|krag]].<ref name="GCG" />

At some point, Frithia was [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] by members of the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from<ref name="CSWE Iridonia">{{CSWECite|II|115|Iridonia}}</ref> the Glythe [[Sector/Legends|sector]]<ref name="EAOC Iridonia">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Iridonia system/Legends|Iridonia system]]</ref> planet [[Iridonia/Legends|Iridonia]].<ref name="CSWE Iridonia" /> Many of the colonists on Frithia were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the local krags, although the creature was eventually domesticated.<ref name="GCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Frithia was first mentioned in [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]] and [[J.D. Wiker]]'s [[2003]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''.<ref name="GCG" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Frithia [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square K-7.<ref name="TEA" />
